steps.title: Compare first-year earnings
steps.content:
Account A earns $6\%$ of $500$ dollars in the first year:
$$0.06\cdot500=30$$
Account B earns a fixed $25$ dollars per year.

steps.title: Use compounding growth
steps.content: Because Account A compounds annually, its balance grows each year, so $6\%$ of that growing balance is more than the first year's $30$ dollars. Account B stays fixed at $25$ dollars per year.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Account A starts above Account B and continues increasing, so Account A always earns more money per year. The answer is A.
